mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls above 100k signals robust labor market conditions, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in < 40% of triggered instances despite payroll growth above threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and industrial production (INDPRO) indicates strong aggregate demand and supply-side capacity, creating a positive feedback loop that drives S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of GDPC1 and INDPRO fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 50% of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) triggers expectations of tighter monetary policy, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances despite CPIAUCSL above 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improved manufacturing activity, which directly boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to produce positive 5d returns in > 40% of cases, or its predictive power drops below 0.15 in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment (UNRATE) below 5% signals tightening labor markets, increasing wage pressures and corporate costs, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances despite UNRATE below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential construction (HOUST) and real savings (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer confidence and housing demand, creating a positive momentum effect that drives S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint growth of HOUST and RSAFS fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 50% of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in unemployment (UNRATE) above 7% signals economic distress, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings, which leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances despite UNRATE above 7%.
